```{r}
# ============================================================
# Graph 3: Stacked Bar Chart of Meteorite Composition by Era
# "What we're finding has shifted too"
# ============================================================

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)

# Count meteorites by time period and type
composition_data <- meteorites_clean %>%
  group_by(time_period, meteorite_type) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(time_period) %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(count),
    proportion = count / total * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Define colors matching your original palette
type_colors <- c(
  "Stone"      = "#2A9D8F",
  "Iron"       = "#E63946",
  "Stony-Iron" = "#F4A261",
  "Other"      = "#999999"
)

# --- Build the Plot ---

ggplot(composition_data, 
       aes(x = time_period, y = proportion, fill = meteorite_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7) +
  # Percentage labels inside each segment (hide if too small)
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(proportion >= 4, 
                        paste0(round(proportion, 1), "%"), 
                        "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    color = "white",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  # Count annotations below each bar
  geom_text(
    data = composition_data %>%
      group_by(time_period) %>%
      summarise(total = first(total), .groups = "drop"),
    aes(x = time_period, y = -3, 
        label = paste0("n = ", format(total, big.mark = ",")),
        fill = NULL),
    size = 3,
    color = "gray50",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Meteorite Type",
    values = type_colors
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.02))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "What We're Finding Has Shifted Too",
    subtitle = "Proportion of meteorite types discovered in each era",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Proportion of discoveries",
    caption = "Data Source: NASA Open Data Portal / The Meteoritical Society (data.nasa.gov)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "gray40", hjust = 0),
    plot.caption  = element_text(size = 8, color = "gray50", hjust = 0),
    axis.text.x   = element_text(size = 9, lineheight = 1.1),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.text     = element_text(size = 9)
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5)))
```

```{r}

# Graph 4: Violin / Box Plot of Mass by Era (Log-Scaled)
# "We're finding smaller meteorites now"


library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)


# Remove rows with missing or zero mass for this plot
mass_data <- meteorites_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(mass..g.) & mass..g. > 0)

# Define a palette for the four time periods
era_colors <- c(
  "1800-1949: Early Discoveries"  = "#E63946",
  "1950-1979: Mid-Century"        = "#F4A261",
  "1980-1999: Modern Era"         = "#2A9D8F",
  "2000-2013: Recent Discoveries" = "#457B9D"
)

# Compute summary statistics for annotation
mass_summary <- mass_data %>%
  group_by(time_period) %>%
  summarise(
    n        = n(),
    median_g = median(mass..g.),
    mean_g   = mean(mass..g.),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

# Violin plot
ggplot(mass_data, aes(x = time_period, y = mass..g., fill = time_period)) +
  # Violin layer — shows full distribution shape
  geom_violin(
    alpha      = 0.35,
    color      = NA,
    scale      = "width",
    trim       = TRUE,
    adjust     = 0.8
  ) +
  # Box plot layer — shows quartiles and median
  geom_boxplot(
    width      = 0.15,
    outlier.shape = NA,
    color      = "gray30",
    fill       = "white",
    alpha      = 0.8
  ) +
  # Median annotation points
  stat_summary(
    fun    = median,
    geom   = "point",
    shape  = 18,
    size   = 3,
    color  = "gray20"
  ) +
  # Median value labels
  geom_text(
    data = mass_summary,
    aes(x = time_period, y = median_g,
        label = ifelse(median_g >= 1000,
                        paste0(round(median_g / 1000, 1), " kg"),
                        paste0(round(median_g), " g"))),
    hjust      = -0.3,
    vjust      = -0.8,
    size       = 3.2,
    fontface   = "bold",
    color      = "gray25",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  # Sample size labels at the bottom
  geom_text(
    data = mass_summary,
    aes(x = time_period, y = 0.05,
        label = paste0("n = ", format(n, big.mark = ","))),
    size       = 3,
    color      = "gray50",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  # Log scale for mass
  scale_y_log10(
    labels = function(x) {
      ifelse(x >= 1e6, paste0(x / 1e6, " t"),
      ifelse(x >= 1000, paste0(x / 1000, " kg"),
             paste0(x, " g")))
    },
    breaks = c(0.1, 1, 10, 100, 1000, 1e4, 1e5, 1e6, 1e7)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = era_colors) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = c(
      "1800-1949: Early Discoveries"  = "1800-1949",
      "1950-1979: Mid-Century"        = "1950-1979",
      "1980-1999: Modern Era"         = "1980-1999",
      "2000-2013: Recent Discoveries" = "2000-2013"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "We're Finding Smaller Meteorites Now",
    subtitle = "Mass distribution of discovered meteorites by era (log scale)\nMedian mass has dropped steadily as systematic searches detect smaller specimens",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Mass (log scale)",
    caption  = "Data Source: NASA Open Data Portal / The Meteoritical Society (data.nasa.gov)\nDiamond = median mass; box = IQR; \nn = number of meteorite discoveries per era; \noutliers suppressed for clarity"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray40", hjust = 0,
                                     lineheight = 1.2),
    plot.caption     = element_text(size = 8, color = "gray50", hjust = 0),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 9, lineheight = 1.1),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "none"
  ) +
  # Reference line at 1 kg for intuitive anchor
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1000, linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "gray70", linewidth = 0.4) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.6, y = 1000, label = "1 kg",
           size = 2.8, color = "gray50", hjust = 0, vjust = -0.5)
```
